info.title: FDO Owner Server API
info.description:
API for the FDO Owner Server role. The Owner Server is responsible for managing
device ownership, including ownership vouchers, trusted device CA certificates,
and rendezvous TO2 addresses for device onboarding.

This specification aggregates the following management APIs:
- Ownership Voucher Management (/v1/vouchers) (Deprecated)
- Ownership Voucher Management (/v2/vouchers)
- Trusted Device CA Certificates (/v1/device-ca) (Deprecated) 
- Trusted Device CA Certificates (/v2/device-ca)
- RVTO2 Address Configuration (/v1/owner/redirect) (Deprecated) 
- RVTO2 Address Configuration (/v2/rvto2addr)

## API Versioning
This API uses semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) and includes the major
version in the URL path (e.g., `/api/v2/vouchers`).

- **MAJOR version** changes indicate breaking changes (incompatible API changes)
- **MINOR version** changes add functionality in a backward-compatible manner
- **PATCH version** changes are backward-compatible bug fixes

### Version Support Policy
- The current major version (v2) is fully supported
- When a new major version is released, the previous major version will be
  supported for a minimum of 12 months
- Deprecated endpoints or fields will be marked in the documentation and
  supported for at least 6 months before removal
- Deprecation notices will include the deprecation date, removal date, and
  migration guidance

### Breaking Changes
Breaking changes that trigger a major version increment include:
- Removing or renaming endpoints
- Removing or renaming request/response fields
- Changing field types or validation rules
- Changing authentication mechanisms

## Pagination
List operations support pagination using `offset` and `limit` query parameters:
- `offset`: Number of items to skip (default: 0, minimum: 0)
- `limit`: Maximum number of items to return (default: 100, minimum: 1, maximum: 1000)

## Rate Limiting
API requests may be subject to rate limiting to ensure service stability.
Rate limit information is provided in response headers when applicable:
- `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Maximum requests allowed in the time window
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Remaining requests in the current window
- `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Time when the rate limit resets (Unix timestamp)

When rate limits are exceeded, the API returns a 429 Too Many Requests status code.

## Request Body Size Limit
All management API endpoints enforce a maximum request body size of 10MB.
Requests exceeding this limit will receive a `413 Payload Too Large` response.

get.description:
Returns the server version and health status.

Note: This endpoint does not require authentication and is intended
for health checks by monitoring systems and load balancers.

Returns a list of ownership vouchers matching optional filter criteria.

Results are ordered by updated_at timestamp in descending order.
The CBOR field is omitted from results (use GET /vouchers/{guid} to retrieve full voucher).

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: Filter by device GUID (hex-encoded)

parameters.name: device_info
parameters.description: Filter by device info string

500.description: Internal server error occurred while querying vouchers

post.description:
Inserts one or more ownership vouchers into the database.

The request body should contain PEM-encoded ownership voucher(s).
Each voucher is verified for:
- Ownership (voucher owner key matches server's configured keys)
- Cryptographic integrity (signature chain, certificate chains, etc.)
- Protocol version and header field validity

Duplicate vouchers (same GUID with same CBOR data) are silently skipped.
Existing vouchers (same GUID with different CBOR data) are not overwritten and are silently skipped.

The operation succeeds (200 OK) even if all vouchers were duplicates/skipped.

application/x-pem-file.example:
-----BEGIN OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
[base64-encoded CBOR data]
-----END OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----

200.description: Voucher(s) successfully inserted or were duplicates

400.description: Invalid voucher data or verification failed

413.description: Request payload too large (exceeds maximum allowed size).

500.description: Internal server error occurred while inserting voucher

readBodyError.value: Failure to read the request body

get.description:
Retrieves a specific ownership voucher by its device GUID.

Returns the voucher in PEM format with full CBOR data.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: Device GUID (hex-encoded, 32 characters)

application/x-pem-file.example:
-----BEGIN OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
[base64-encoded CBOR data]
-----END OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----

post.summary: Resell ownership voucher to new owner (Deprecated)
post.description:
Extends an ownership voucher to a new owner by adding a new entry to the signature chain.

This operation:
1. Retrieves the current voucher by GUID
2. Extends it with the new owner's public key
3. Deletes the old voucher
4. Inserts the extended voucher
5. Returns the extended voucher in PEM format

The operation is atomic (wrapped in a database transaction).
If any step fails, the transaction is rolled back and the original voucher is preserved.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: Device GUID (hex-encoded, 32 characters)

requestBody.description: PEM-encoded public key of the new owner

application/x-pem-file.example:
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
[base64-encoded public key]
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

200.description: Successfully resold voucher to new owner

application/x-pem-file.example:
-----BEGIN OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
[base64-encoded extended CBOR data]
-----END OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----

400.description: Invalid GUID, request body, or unsupported key type

invalidGuid.value: GUID is not a valid GUID

404.description: Voucher not found for the specified GUID

text/plain.example: Voucher not found for the specified GUID

413.description: Request payload too large (exceeds maximum allowed size).

500.description: Internal server error occurred during resell operation

readBodyError.value: Failure to read the request body

get.summary: Get the current owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration (Deprecated)
get.description:
Retrieves the current rendezvous TO2 (Transfer of Ownership 2) address
configuration that specifies where devices should connect to the owner server
after completing the rendezvous protocol.

(Deprecated: please use "/rvto2addr" endpoint instead)

200.description: Successfully retrieved the owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration.

httpsWithBothDnsAndIP.summary: HTTPS configuration with both DNS and IP
httpsWithBothDnsAndIP.description: Both dns and ip can be provided together

multipleAddresses.summary: Multiple addresses with primary and backup

404.description: No RVTO2Addr configuration has been created yet.

500.description: Internal server error occurred while fetching the configuration.

post.summary: Create the owner redirect info (RVTOAddr) configuration (Deprecated)
post.description:
Creates the owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration.

The new configuration will be used by devices completing the TO2 protocol
to determine where to connect to the owner server. This typically includes
the owner server's hostname/IP address, protocol, and port.

(Deprecated: please use "/rvto2addr" endpoint instead)

requestBody.description: The new owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration

createMultipleAddresses.summary: Create with primary and backup addresses

createWithBothDnsAndIP.summary: Create with both DNS and IP address
createWithBothDnsAndIP.description: Demonstrates that both dns and ip can be provided together

201.description: Successfully created the owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration

400.description: Invalid owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration provided in the request body.

409.description: Owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration already exists. Use PUT to update instead.

413.description: Request payload too large (exceeds maximum allowed size).

500.description: Internal server error occurred while creating the configuration.

put.summary: Update the owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration (Deprecated)
put.description:
Updates the owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration.

The new configuration will be used by devices completing the TO2 protocol
to determine where to connect to the owner server. This typically includes
the owner server's hostname/IP address, protocol, and port.

(Deprecated: please use "/rvto2addr" endpoint instead)

requestBody.description: The new owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration

updateToMultipleAddresses.summary: Update with primary and backup addresses

updateWithBothDnsAndIP.summary: Update with both DNS and IP address
updateWithBothDnsAndIP.description: Demonstrates that both dns and ip can be provided together

200.description: Successfully updated the owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration

400.description: Invalid owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration provided in the request body.

404.description: Owner redirect info (RVTO2Addr) configuration does not exist. Use POST to create it first.

413.description: Request payload too large (exceeds maximum allowed size).

500.description: Internal server error occurred while updating the configuration.

get.summary: List all trusted device CA certificates
get.description:
Retrieves a paginated, filterable, and sortable list of all registered trusted device CA certificates.

Use the Accept header to specify the response format:
- `application/json` - Returns certificate metadata as JSON (default)
- `application/x-pem-file` - Returns certificates as concatenated PEM file

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: issuer
parameters.description: Filters the results by the exact issuer distinguished name.

schema.example: CN=Example Device CA, O=Example Inc, C=US

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Filters the results by the exact subject distinguished name.

schema.example: CN=Example Device CA, O=Example Inc, C=US

parameters.name: validityStatus
parameters.description: Filters certificates by their validity status.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Searches within subject and issuer fields (case-insensitive).

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sorts the results by the specified field.

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: Specifies the sorting order.

200.description: A paginated list of trusted device CA certificates.

certs.subject: CN=Example Device CA One, O=Example Corp, C=US
certs.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

certs.subject: CN=Example Device CA Two, O=Example Corp, C=US
certs.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

getPEMCACerts.value:
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID... (truncated) ...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID... (truncated) ...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

post.summary: Import one or more trusted device CA certificates
post.description:
Uploads one or more PEM-encoded certificates for trusted device certificate authorities.
A single PEM file may contain multiple certificates.

This operation is idempotent:
- Valid, non-expired certificates that don't exist in the database are imported
- Certificates that already exist (duplicate fingerprint) are silently skipped
- Expired certificates are silently skipped
- Malformed or invalid certificates are silently skipped

The response includes statistics and detailed messages about the operation:

- `detected`:  Total number of parsable certificate blocks found in the PEM data.
- `malformed`: Number of PEM blocks that could not be parsed as valid certificates.
- `imported`:  Number of new, valid certificates successfully imported into the database.
- `skipped`:   Number of parsable certificates that were not imported. This includes certificates that were expired or already exist in the database.
- `messages`:  Array of detailed status messages for each certificate processed

requestBody.description: One or more PEM-encoded certificates. The file may contain multiple certificates.

createCert.summary: Example of creating new CA certificates
createCert.value:
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID... (truncated) ...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID... (truncated) ...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

200.description: Certificate upload processed successfully. Returns statistics and details of imported certificates.

messages: the certificate at position 1 with subject 'CN=Example Device CA One, O=Example Corp, C=US' was imported successfully
the certificate at position 2 with subject 'CN=Example Device CA Two, O=Example Corp, C=US' was imported successfully

certs.subject: CN=Example Device CA One, O=Example Corp, C=US
certs.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

certs.subject: CN=Example Device CA Two, O=Example Corp, C=US
certs.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

partialImport.summary: Some certificates skipped (duplicates, expired or malformed)

messages: the certificate at position 1 is malformed
the certificate at position 2 with subject 'CN=Example Device CA One, O=Example Corp, C=US' was imported successfully
the certificate at position 3 with subject 'CN=Example Device CA Expired, O=Example Corp, C=US' was skipped because it is expired
the certificate at position 4 with subject 'CN=Example Device CA Two, O=Example Corp, C=US' was imported successfully

certs.subject: CN=Example Device CA One, O=Example Corp, C=US
certs.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

certs.subject: CN=Example Device CA Two, O=Example Corp, C=US
certs.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

messages: the certificate at position 1 with subject 'CN=Example Device CA One, O=Example Corp, C=US' was skipped because it already exists
the certificate at position 2 with subject 'CN=Example Device CA Two, O=Example Corp, C=US' was skipped because it already exists

413.description: Request payload too large (exceeds maximum allowed size).

get.summary: Get a trusted device CA certificate by fingerprint
get.description:
Retrieves a specific trusted device CA certificate by its SHA-256 fingerprint.

Use the Accept header to specify the response format:
- `application/json` - Returns certificate metadata as JSON (default)
- `application/x-pem-file` - Returns certificate as PEM file

parameters.name: fingerprint
parameters.description: The SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate (64 hexadecimal characters).

200.description: The requested trusted device CA certificate.

value.subject: CN=Example Device CA, O=Example Corp, C=US
value.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

schema.description: The certificate content in PEM format.

delete.summary: Delete a trusted device CA certificate by fingerprint
delete.description: Deletes a specific trusted device CA certificate by its SHA-256 fingerprint.

parameters.name: fingerprint
parameters.description: The SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate (64 hexadecimal characters).

204.description: Trusted device CA certificate deleted successfully.

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated, filterable, and sortable list of all ownership vouchers.

Use the Accept header to specify the response format:
- `application/json` - Returns voucher metadata as JSON (default)
- `application/x-pem-file` - Returns vouchers as concatenated PEM file

If no Accept header is provided, the response defaults to `application/json`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: Filters the results by the exact device GUID.

parameters.name: deviceInfo
parameters.description: Filters the results by device information (case-insensitive partial match).

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Searches within device info and GUID fields (case-insensitive).

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sorts the results by the specified field.

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: Specifies the sorting order.

200.description: A paginated list of ownership vouchers.

success.description:
Shows page 1 of results (items 0-19 of 2 total).
To navigate pages:
- Next page: GET /vouchers?limit=20&offset=20
- Previous page: GET /vouchers?limit=20&offset=0 (already at first page)
- Total pages: ceil(total / limit) = ceil(2 / 20) = 1

getPEMVouchers.value:
-----BEGIN OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
oWRndWlkUNIdhBo/VPTompDtm5d56egEaGVhZGVyWEKhBRkBAQZYPKEBGQEB
AligGdpx5PoC1AO8aTU8kz1mJ9n1KZQ2b6xLvE/sWmPRBIVcEU3hhcmUg
...
-----END OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
-----BEGIN OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
oWRndWlkUKEb7z1bVQ1SjsqJD8yJsd7pgxEaGVhZGVyWEKhBRkBAQZYPKEB
GQEBAKAZ2nHk+gLUA7xpNTyTPWYn2fUplDZvrEu8T+xaY9EEhVwRTeGFy
...
-----END OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----

post.description:
Imports one or more ownership vouchers into the system.

This operation is idempotent:
- Valid vouchers that don't exist in the database are imported
- Vouchers that already exist (duplicate GUID) are rejected with a 409 error
- Malformed or invalid vouchers are rejected with a 400 error

Ownership vouchers can be provided in PEM or JSON format.
You can import a single voucher or an array of vouchers.

requestBody.description: One or more ownership vouchers in PEM or JSON format.

importFromPEM.summary: Example of importing vouchers from PEM
importFromPEM.value:
-----BEGIN OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
oWRndWlkUNIdhBo/VPTompDtm5d56egEaGVhZGVyWEKhBRkBAQZYPKEBGQEB
AligGdpx5PoC1AO8aTU8kz1mJ9n1KZQ2b6xLvE/sWmPRBIVcEU3hhcmUg
...
-----END OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
-----BEGIN OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----
oWRndWlkUKEb7z1bVQ1SjsqJD8yJsd7pgxEaGVhZGVyWEKhBRkBAQZYPKEB
GQEBAKAZ2nHk+gLUA7xpNTyTPWYn2fUplDZvrEu8T+xaY9EEhVwRTeGFy
...
-----END OWNERSHIP VOUCHER-----

409.description: Conflict - A voucher with this GUID already exists.

get.summary: Get an ownership voucher by GUID
get.description:
Retrieves a specific ownership voucher by its device GUID.

Use the Accept header to specify the response format:
- `application/json` - Returns voucher metadata as JSON (default)
- `application/x-pem-file` - Returns voucher as PEM file

If no Accept header is provided, the response defaults to `application/json`.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The device GUID (32 hexadecimal characters).

schema.description: The voucher content in PEM format.

delete.summary: Delete an ownership voucher by GUID
delete.description: Deletes a specific ownership voucher by its device GUID.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The device GUID (32 hexadecimal characters).

post.description:
Extends an ownership voucher by adding a new ownership entry. This operation
is used to transfer ownership of a device from one owner to another.

The request must include the new owner's public key and will be signed
with the current owner's private key.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: The device GUID (32 hexadecimal characters).

requestBody.description: New owner's public key or certificate in PEM format.

extendWithPublicKey.summary: Extend voucher with PEM encoded public key

extendWithCertificate.summary: Extend voucher with PEM encoded certificate

schema.description: The extended voucher in PEM format.

post.summary: Import one or more trusted device CA certificates
post.description:
Uploads one or more PEM-encoded certificates for trusted device certificate authorities.
A single PEM file may contain multiple certificates.

This operation is idempotent:
- Valid, non-expired certificates that don't exist in the database are imported
- Certificates that already exist (duplicate fingerprint) are silently skipped
- Expired certificates are silently skipped
- Malformed or invalid certificates are silently skipped

The response includes statistics and detailed messages about the operation:

- `detected`:  Total number of parsable certificate blocks found in the PEM data.
- `malformed`: Number of PEM blocks that could not be parsed as valid certificates.
- `imported`:  Number of new, valid certificates successfully imported into the database.
- `skipped`:   Number of parsable certificates that were not imported. This includes certificates that were expired or already exist in the database.
- `messages`:  Array of detailed status messages for each certificate processed

requestBody.description: One or more PEM-encoded certificates. The file may contain multiple certificates.

createCert.value:
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID... (truncated) ...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID... (truncated) ...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----

200.description: Certificate upload processed successfully. Returns statistics and details of imported certificates.

get.summary: Get a trusted device CA certificate by fingerprint
get.description:
Retrieves a specific trusted device CA certificate by its SHA-256 fingerprint.

Use the Accept header to specify the response format:
- `application/json` - Returns certificate metadata as JSON (default)
- `application/x-pem-file` - Returns certificate as PEM file

parameters.name: fingerprint
parameters.description: The SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate (64 hexadecimal characters).

200.description: The requested trusted device CA certificate.

value.subject: CN=Example Device CA, O=Example Corp, C=US
value.issuer: CN=Example Intermediate CA, O=Example Corp, C=US

schema.description: The certificate content in PEM format.

delete.summary: Delete a trusted device CA certificate by fingerprint
delete.description: Deletes a specific trusted device CA certificate by its SHA-256 fingerprint.

parameters.name: fingerprint
parameters.description: The SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate (64 hexadecimal characters).

204.description: Trusted device CA certificate deleted successfully.

get.summary: Get the current RVTO2 address configuration
get.description:
Retrieves the current rendezvous TO2 (Transfer of Ownership 2) address
configuration that specifies where devices should connect to the owner server
after completing the rendezvous protocol.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the RVTO2 address configuration. Returns an empty array if no configuration has been set.

httpsWithBothDnsAndIP.summary: HTTPS configuration with both DNS and IP
httpsWithBothDnsAndIP.description: Both dns and ip can be provided together

multipleAddresses.summary: Multiple addresses with primary and backup

put.description:
Updates the rendezvous TO2 address configuration.

The new configuration will be used by devices completing the TO2 protocol
to determine where to connect to the owner server. This typically includes
the owner server's hostname/IP address, protocol, and port.

updateToMultipleAddresses.summary: Update with primary and backup addresses

updateWithBothDnsAndIP.summary: Update with both DNS and IP address
updateWithBothDnsAndIP.description: Demonstrates that both dns and ip can be provided together

200.description: Successfully updated the RVTO2 address configuration

delete.description:
Deletes the current rendezvous TO2 address configuration.

After deletion, devices will not have owner server address information
available until a new configuration is set via PUT. Returns the deleted
configuration, or an empty array if no configuration was set.

200.description: Successfully deleted the RVTO2 address configuration. Returns the deleted configuration or an empty array if nothing was configured.

message.description: Human-readable status message or error description
message.example: the service is up and running

created_at.description: Timestamp when voucher was first inserted

updated_at.description: Timestamp when voucher was last updated

TransportProtocol.description:
Transport protocol for TO2 owner server connections
(FDO specification section 3.3.14, TransportProtocol).
- `http` - HTTP protocol (code 3)
- `https` - HTTPS protocol (code 5)
- `tcp` - Raw TCP connection (code 1)
- `tls` - TLS-secured TCP connection (code 2)
- `coap` - CoAP over UDP (code 4)
- `rest` - RESTful HTTP/HTTPS (mapped to HTTPS)

RVTO2AddrEntry.description:
A single owner server address entry. Each entry specifies the protocol,
network location (DNS hostname and/or IP address), and port for connecting
to the owner server during TO2 protocol.

Both 'dns' and 'ip' fields are optional and may be provided independently
or together. However, at least one addressing method MUST be specified
(non-null) as required by FDO specification section 3.3.14. Requests that
omit both 'dns' and 'ip' will be rejected with a 400 Bad Request error.

When both 'dns' and 'ip' are provided, the device MUST attempt to connect
using the DNS address first. The device MUST only use the IP address after
all DNS connection attempts have failed. This behavior is defined in the
FDO specification section 3.3.14.

RVTO2Addr.description:
Rendezvous TO2 address configuration. Specifies one or more owner server
addresses that devices should use to connect during the TO2 (Transfer of
Ownership 2) protocol after completing rendezvous. Multiple entries provide
fallback options if the primary address is unreachable.

fingerprint.description: The SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate (64 hexadecimal characters).

pem.description: The full certificate content in PEM format.

subject.description: The subject distinguished name of the certificate.

issuer.description: The issuer distinguished name of the certificate.

notBefore.description: The start date of the certificate's validity period.

notAfter.description: The end date of the certificate's validity period.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the certificate was added.

total.description: Total number of trusted device CA certificates available.

limit.description: The number of items returned in this response.

offset.description: The offset from the beginning of the collection.

detected.description: Total number of certificate blocks found in the PEM data.

imported.description: Number of certificates successfully added to the database.

skipped.description: Number of parsable certificates that were not imported. This includes certificates that were expired or already exist in the database.

malformed.description: Number of certificate blocks that could not be parsed or validated.

messages.description: Detailed import result messages for each certificate found in the PEM data.
example: the certificate at position 1 is malformed
the certificate at position 2 with subject 'CN=Device CA 2,O=Example Inc' was imported successfully
the certificate at position 3 with subject 'CN=Device CA 3,O=Example Inc' was skipped because it is expired
the certificate at position 4 with subject 'CN=Device CA 4,O=Example Inc' was skipped because it already exists
the certificate at position 5 with subject 'CN=Device CA 5,O=Example Inc' was imported successfully

certs.description: Array of certificates that were successfully imported (does not include malformed, expired or already existing certificates).

VoucherProtocolVersion.description: The FDO protocol version (e.g., 101 for version 1.1).

VoucherNumEntries.description: Number of ownership entries in the voucher.

OwnershipVoucherCreatedAt.description: The timestamp when the voucher was created.

OwnershipVoucherUpdatedAt.description: The timestamp when the voucher was last updated.

total.description: Total number of ownership vouchers available.

limit.description: The number of items returned in this response.

offset.description: The offset from the beginning of the collection.

RVProtocol.description:
Rendezvous protocol value (FDO specification Table 3.6, RVProtocolValue).
Specifies the protocol for communication with the rendezvous server:
- `rest` - RESTful HTTP/HTTPS (code 0)
- `http` - HTTP protocol (code 1)
- `https` - HTTPS protocol (code 2)
- `tcp` - Raw TCP connection (code 3)
- `tls` - TLS-secured TCP connection (code 4)
- `coap+tcp` - CoAP over TCP (code 5)
- `coap` - CoAP over UDP (code 6)

Medium.description:
Network medium/interface to use for communication:
- `wired_1` through `wired_9` - Specific wired network interfaces
- `wifi_1` through `wifi_9` - Specific WiFi network interfaces
- `wired_all` - Any available wired interface
- `wifi_all` - Any available WiFi interface

DevicePort.description: Port number on the device side

OwnerPort.description: Port number on the owner/server side

DevOnly.description: Instruction applies to device only (not owner)

OwnerOnly.description: Instruction applies to owner only (not device)

RVBypass.description: Bypass rendezvous server and connect directly to owner

DelaySeconds.description: Delay in seconds before attempting connection (with up to 25% randomization)

SvCertHash.description: Server certificate hash for validation (SHA-256 or SHA-384)

ClCertHash.description: Client certificate hash for validation (SHA-256 or SHA-384)

UserInput.description: Indicates whether user input is allowed to update rendezvous variables during device onboarding

ExtRV.description: Extended rendezvous information for custom device-specific instructions

RVInfo.description:
Rendezvous information array containing one or more instruction sets
(RendezvousDirective). Each instruction set guides devices and owners on how
to connect to the rendezvous server, including network configuration, protocols,
and connection parameters.

Processing behavior (FDO specification section 3.7):
- Instruction sets are processed in order (index 0 first, then 1, etc.)
- The first successful connection to a rendezvous server MAY be used
- All DNS addresses within an instruction set MUST be processed before
  moving to the next instruction set
- Multiple instruction sets provide fallback options for device connectivity
- Devices and owners may avoid redundant operations (e.g., contacting the
  same IP address twice)

value.description:
Hexadecimal hash value. The length must match the algorithm:
- SHA-256/HMAC-SHA-256: 64 hexadecimal characters (32 bytes)
- SHA-384/HMAC-SHA-384: 96 hexadecimal characters (48 bytes)
- SHA-512/HMAC-SHA-512: 128 hexadecimal characters (64 bytes)

certChainHash.description: Hash of the certificate chain (required when certChain is present).

previousHash.description: Hash of the previous entry (all zeros for first entry).

certChain.description: X.509 certificate chain in PEM format (null for Intel EPID).

fingerprint.description: The SHA-256 fingerprint of the certificate (64 hexadecimal characters).

pem.description: The full certificate content in PEM format.

subject.description: The subject distinguished name of the certificate.

issuer.description: The issuer distinguished name of the certificate.

notBefore.description: The start date of the certificate's validity period.

notAfter.description: The end date of the certificate's validity period.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the certificate was added.

total.description: Total number of trusted device CA certificates available.

limit.description: The number of items returned in this response.

offset.description: The offset from the beginning of the collection.

detected.description: Total number of certificate blocks found in the PEM data.

imported.description: Number of certificates successfully added to the database.

skipped.description: Number of parsable certificates that were not imported. This includes certificates that were expired or already exist in the database.

malformed.description: Number of certificate blocks that could not be parsed or validated.

messages.description: Detailed import result messages for each certificate found in the PEM data.
example: the certificate at position 1 is malformed
the certificate at position 2 with subject 'CN=Device CA 2,O=Example Inc' was imported successfully
the certificate at position 3 with subject 'CN=Device CA 3,O=Example Inc' was skipped because it is expired
the certificate at position 4 with subject 'CN=Device CA 4,O=Example Inc' was skipped because it already exists
the certificate at position 5 with subject 'CN=Device CA 5,O=Example Inc' was imported successfully

certs.description: Array of certificates that were successfully imported (does not include malformed, expired or already existing certificates).

TransportProtocol1.description:
Transport protocol for TO2 owner server connections
(FDO specification section 3.3.14, TransportProtocol).
- `http` - HTTP protocol (code 3)
- `https` - HTTPS protocol (code 5)
- `tcp` - Raw TCP connection (code 1)
- `tls` - TLS-secured TCP connection (code 2)
- `coap` - CoAP over UDP (code 4)
- `rest` - RESTful HTTP/HTTPS (mapped to HTTPS)

RVTO2AddrEntry1.description:
A single owner server address entry. Each entry specifies the protocol,
network location (DNS hostname and/or IP address), and port for connecting
to the owner server during TO2 protocol.

Both 'dns' and 'ip' fields are optional and may be provided independently
or together. However, at least one addressing method MUST be specified
(non-null) as required by FDO specification section 3.3.14. Requests that
omit both 'dns' and 'ip' will be rejected with a 400 Bad Request error.

When both 'dns' and 'ip' are provided, the device MUST attempt to connect
using the DNS address first. The device MUST only use the IP address after
all DNS connection attempts have failed. This behavior is defined in the
FDO specification section 3.3.14.

RVTO2Addr1.description:
Rendezvous TO2 address configuration. Specifies one or more owner server
addresses that devices should use to connect during the TO2 (Transfer of
Ownership 2) protocol after completing rendezvous. Multiple entries provide
fallback options if the primary address is unreachable.

value.message: the service is up and running

BadRequest.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or invalid.

badRequest.summary: Example of a bad request error

NotFound.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist.

notFound.summary: Example of a not found error

BadRequest1.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or invalid.

badRequest.summary: Example of a bad request error

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Authentication credentials missing, invalid, or expired.

value.message: Authentication token is invalid or has expired.

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - The authenticated user does not have permission to perform this action.

value.message: User does not have the right access level.

PayloadTooLarge.description: Payload Too Large - The request body exceeds the maximum allowed size (1MB).

payloadTooLarge.summary: Example of a payload too large error

NotFound1.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist.

notFound.summary: Example of a not found error
